# javaBin — The Early Years: An Archive

> Documentary archive of javaBin, the Norwegian Java User Group (founded 1998), and its evolution into JavaZone — Scandinavia's largest developer conference.

## What This Is

This site chronicles the founding and early years of javaBin (java.no) from 1996 to 2008, built from primary sources: firsthand accounts from the founders, Wayback Machine snapshots of the original java.no site, annual meeting records, and community tribute posts.

**Live site:** https://totto.github.io/javabin-archieve/
**Repository:** https://github.com/totto/javabin-archieve

## Structured Navigation (for AI agents)

This repository publishes a KCP manifest at https://totto.github.io/javabin-archieve/knowledge.yaml
listing all research units with intent declarations.

## Key Historical Facts

- **April 23, 1998** — The founding meeting, organized by Markus Harboe at Institutt for Informatikk (IFI), Universitetet i Oslo
- **Thor Henning Hetland (Totto)** — arrived at the second meeting, elected president; led javaBin for ~10 years
- **Motivation** — Totto was shocked by the low level of programming knowledge in Norwegian businesses when he started as a consultant at Taskon after finishing NTH/NTNU; javaBin was a reaction to that gap
- **java.no** — the community's web platform, a Java web application run on Solaris; later migrated to Linux; forum ran JForum (open-source, customized by Totto)
- **JavaZone** — first held 2002 (350 attendees), grew to 3,600+ at Oslo Spektrum (2024)
- **Markus Harboe** — the real founder; organized the April 23 founding meeting; later moved from Java development into cybersecurity (CISSP, now at mnemonic AS)
- **Trygve Reenskaug** — inventor of the MVC architectural pattern (Numerica Taskon) — was listed as a javaBin hero in 2001
- **Duke's Choice Award** — received 2019 for JavaZone

## People (Key)

| Person | Role |
|--------|------|
| Thor Henning Hetland (Totto) | President 1998–2007 (approx.), co-founder of JavaZone |
| Markus Harboe | Organized the founding meeting (April 23, 1998) |
| Stein Grimstad | One of the three pillars (Totto, Stein, Carl) per Olve Maudal's 2007 tribute |
| Carl Onstad | One of the three pillars |
| Olve Maudal | Wrote the definitive 2007 tribute post; later became a prominent javaBin presenter |
| Trygve Reenskaug | MVC inventor, javaBin hero from the founding era |

## Content Index

**Site (docs/):**
- `index.html` — Single-page documentary covering 8 sections: origin, people, platform, JavaZone, community, handover, legacy, sources

**Research baseline (baseline/):**
- `baseline-report.md` — Main research report: origins, timeline, key people, JavaZone history
- `pre-cibus-era.md` — Technical infrastructure pre-Cibus: java.no stack, JForum, Solaris
- `additional-findings.md` — Post-Totto leadership, financials, GitHub repos, board composition
- `technical-infrastructure.md` — Detailed tech stack: Cibus CMS, Confluence wiki, server evolution
- `known-people.csv` — 29 identified individuals with roles and years active
- `board-history.csv` — Board composition by year
- `javazone-timeline.csv` — JavaZone year-by-year: venue, attendance, theme

**Primary sources (sources/):**
- `totto-firsthand-memories.md` — Totto's own words (April 2026): founding, motivation, Markus Harboe, programming since age 10
- `olve-maudal-tribute-2007.md` — Olve Maudal's tribute naming the three pillars
- `jz09-official-backstory.md` — Official JavaZone 2009 backstory; confirms April 23, 1998 founding date

**Wayback snapshots (snapshots/):**
- `homepage-timeline.md` — java.no homepage captures 1997–2006 with extracted Norwegian text
- `forum-snapshots.md` — Forum snapshots: 1999 nav structure, 2005 JForum platform confirmation

## Tech

Plain HTML + CSS. No build step, no JavaScript frameworks. Deploys on GitHub Pages from docs/.
Research files are Markdown and CSV — human and machine readable.
